evals.prompt: I have a registration form with an age field. The valid age range is 18 to 60. Help me create test cases for this field.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that age is ambiguous — could be whole years (integer) or birthdate-calculated — and asks the user to clarify the precision before generating test cases.
assertions: The output asks the user to clarify the minimum unit or precision of age (e.g. whole years vs birthdate with year-month-day)
The output explains how different precision levels lead to different boundary values
The output does not generate a final BVA table before the user confirms the precision

evals.prompt: Our booking system accepts appointments between 09:00 and 17:00. Help me create test cases for the time input.
evals.expected_output: BVA test cases for time range with columns ID, Name, Description, Input, Expected Output covering boundary values 08:59, 09:00, 09:01, 16:59, 17:00, 17:01.
assertions: The output includes time boundary values 08:59, 09:00, 09:01, 16:59, 17:00, 17:01
The table has columns: ID, Name, Description, Input, Expected Output
Each Description is written in business-understandable language
The Input column shows concrete time values
The Expected Output column shows Valid or Invalid (or equivalent pass/fail)

evals.prompt: I have an input field for "comments". Help me create BVA test cases.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes that a free-text field lacks numeric or time boundaries and asks for clarification or suggests alternative techniques.
assertions: The output recognizes that a free-text field is not suitable for BVA
The output asks for clarification or suggests alternative testing techniques

evals.prompt: The score field must be at least 0. Generate BVA test cases.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects only a lower limit (minimum 0) with no upper limit, asks the user to confirm or provide the upper limit before generating test cases.
assertions: The output detects that only a lower limit (0) is provided
The output asks the user to confirm or provide the missing upper limit
The output does not generate a full BVA table without user confirmation

evals.prompt: The discount percentage cannot exceed 50%. Create BVA test cases for this field.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects only an upper limit (maximum 50) with no lower limit, asks the user to confirm or provide the lower limit before generating test cases.
assertions: The output detects that only an upper limit (50) is provided
The output asks the user to confirm or provide the missing lower limit
The output does not generate a full BVA table without user confirmation

evals.prompt: Salary field accepts values from 15000 to 200000 (integer, Thai Baht). Generate BVA test cases with test data I can use immediately.
evals.expected_output: A table with columns ID, Name, Description, Input, Expected Output covering boundary values 14999, 15000, 15001, 199999, 200000, 200001 with business-friendly descriptions.
assertions: The output includes boundary values 14999, 15000, 15001, 199999, 200000, 200001
The table has columns: ID, Name, Description, Input, Expected Output
Each Name clearly states what the test case covers (e.g. below minimum salary, minimum salary boundary)
Each Description explains in business language what is being tested and what should happen
The Input column contains concrete numeric values ready to copy-paste
The Expected Output column clearly indicates whether the system should accept or reject the value

evals.prompt: Registration requires users to be not over 65 years old. Create BVA test cases for age validation.
evals.expected_output: The skill asks the user to clarify the minimum unit of age data — is it measured in whole years only (e.g. age 64 vs 65 vs 66), or in years-months-days via birthdate (e.g. 64y 11m 29d)? This determines whether the input field is age-as-integer or birthdate.
assertions: The output asks the user to clarify the minimum unit or precision of age (e.g. whole years vs birthdate with year-month-day)
The output explains how different precision levels lead to different boundary values
The output does not generate a final BVA table before the user confirms the precision

evals.prompt: Bank account has a daily deposit limit of not over 30,000 baht. Generate BVA test cases for the deposit amount field.
evals.expected_output: The skill asks the user to clarify the minimum unit of money — is it satang (0.01), whole baht (1), or larger denominations like 100 baht? This determines whether the boundary below 30,000 is 29,999.99, 29,999, or 29,900.
assertions: The output asks the user to clarify the minimum unit or precision of the deposit amount
The output gives examples of how different units produce different boundary values (e.g. 29,999.99 for satang vs 29,999 for whole baht vs 29,900 for 100-baht units)
The output does not generate a final BVA table before the user confirms the precision
